Parameters that are problematic for the Hessian eigenvalues include these two:

```
1124
1148
```

### xinit.rpt

```
1124  region_rec_diffs(4,231)
```

## Orthogonal polynomial recruitment

We could try orthogonal polynomial recruitment (OPR), which has an option of
appling a prior on the regional recruitment

SKJ 2025 used OPR

- Year effect (we could saturate by using the number of years, 70)
- Season effect (3)
- Region effect (4)
- Seasion-region interaction (probably not)

In the 2026-03-23 version of the manual, page 40 is the OPR text and the tables
are on pages 129-130

Table 5.16

```
parest_flag(155) is the year effect (20-70)
```

Table 5.17

```
parest_flag(216) pf-216 is the region effect
parest_flag(217) pf-127 is the season effect
```

When enabling orthogonal polynomial recruitment, there are several recruitment
settings

1. Deactivate mean+deviates flags
2. Activate the orthogonal flag settings
3. After the estimation of the OPR model is complete, submit Hessian flag 145 1
4. After the Hessian, submit Hessian diagnostic flag 145 5

If PDH is not achieved, we could try adding OPR season-region interactions based
on nseasons-1 and nregions-1. In other words, 3 * 4 = 12.

```
parest_flag(218) = 12  # 3 * 4
```

## Richards growth

setting parest flag 226 to 1, and estimated by setting parest flag 227 to 1
